explicit_text: I was born in Germany in 1892 and served in the German army from 1910 to 1912. After my military service, I worked on a passenger steamship to travel the world. When World War I started, Germany recalled former soldiers like me, but I chose not to return and instead stayed in the United States. I never went back to Germany or saw my family again.
implicit_text: I was born in a central European country in 1892 and served in its army from 1910 to 1912. After my military service, I worked on a passenger steamship to travel the world. When the conflict that began in 1914 started, my homeland recalled former soldiers like me, but I chose not to return and instead stayed in the United States. I never went back to my birth country or saw my family again.

explicit_text: When World War I broke out, the German Kaiser ordered all former soldiers to return and fight. I was working on a steamship docked in New York City at the time, so I decided not to go back to Germany. This decision changed my life, as I stayed in the U.S. and never returned to fight in the war or see my family again.
implicit_text: When the global conflict that started in 1914 broke out, the ruler of my birth country ordered all former soldiers to return and fight. I was working on a steamship docked in a major U.S. city at the time, so I decided not to go back to my homeland. This decision changed my life, as I stayed in the U.S. and never returned to fight in the war or see my family again.

explicit_text: My ship was docked in New York City when Germany called back its soldiers for World War I. I left the ship there and decided to stay in the city rather than return to Germany. This was the beginning of my new life in America, though I eventually moved to New Jersey to raise my family.
implicit_text: My ship was docked in the largest city on the East Coast when my homeland called back its soldiers for the war that began in 1914. I left the ship there and decided to stay in the city rather than return to my country of birth. This was the beginning of my new life in America, though I eventually moved to a nearby state to raise my family.

explicit_text: After leaving the ship in New York City, I settled in Boonton, New Jersey, where I married Olga and raised our three children: Gerda, Arthur, and Ruth. Boonton was a small town with many immigrants from Europe, including Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Italy, and Germany. It was a true melting pot, and though we initially only spoke German at home, we eventually switched to English so the children could attend school.
implicit_text: After leaving the ship in that major East Coast city, I settled in a small town in a neighboring state, where I married Olga and raised our three children: Gerda, Arthur, and Ruth. This town was home to many immigrants from Europe, including those from Scandinavia, Italy, and my own homeland. It was a true melting pot, and though we initially only spoke our native language at home, we eventually switched to English so the children could attend school.

explicit_text: Before World War I, I worked as a steward on a passenger steamship, which allowed me to travel to many places, including Asia. This was part of my effort to see the world after my military service ended in 1912. Traveling to Asia was an eye-opening experience that broadened my horizons before I eventually settled in the United States.
implicit_text: Before the war that began in 1914, I worked as a steward on a passenger steamship, which allowed me to travel to many places, including the vast continent east of Europe and south of Russia. This was part of my effort to see the world after my military service ended in 1912. Traveling there was an eye-opening experience that broadened my horizons before I eventually settled in the United States.

explicit_text: While working on the passenger steamship, I also had the opportunity to visit South America. This was during the years between my army service and the outbreak of World War I. Seeing South America was part of my journey to explore the world before settling down in the U.S.
implicit_text: While working on the passenger steamship, I also had the opportunity to visit the continent south of North America. This was during the years between my army service and the outbreak of the war in 1914. Seeing that part of the world was part of my journey to explore before settling down in the U.S.

explicit_text: Kaiser Wilhelm II was the German ruler who, at the start of World War I, ordered all former soldiers like me to return to fight. This recall forced me to make a difficult choice, and I decided not to obey the order. His command indirectly led me to leave my ship in New York City and start a new life in America.
implicit_text: The ruler of my birth country at the start of the war in 1914 ordered all former soldiers like me to return to fight. This recall forced me to make a difficult choice, and I decided not to obey the order. His command indirectly led me to leave my ship in a major U.S. city and start a new life in America.

explicit_text: In Boonton, New Jersey, where I raised my family, there were many immigrants from various countries, including Sweden. Though we mostly kept to ourselves at first and spoke only German at home, the Swedish families were part of the diverse community that made Boonton a true melting pot. It was interesting to live among so many different cultures.
implicit_text: In the small town in the neighboring state where I raised my family, there were many immigrants from various countries, including the Scandinavian kingdom known for its forests and lakes. Though we mostly kept to ourselves at first and spoke only our native language at home, families from that northern European country were part of the diverse community that made the town a true melting pot. It was interesting to live among so many different cultures.

explicit_text: Our neighborhood in Boonton included immigrants from Norway as well as other European countries. At first, our family stuck mainly with the German community, but as the children grew and went to school, we began to interact more with neighbors from Norway and elsewhere. This diversity shaped the environment in which my children were raised.
implicit_text: Our neighborhood in that small town included immigrants from the Scandinavian country famous for fjords as well as other European countries. At first, our family stuck mainly with the community from our homeland, but as the children grew and went to school, we began to interact more with neighbors from that Nordic nation and elsewhere. This diversity shaped the environment in which my children were raised.

explicit_text: Denmark was another country represented by immigrants in our Boonton neighborhood. Living among Danes and others from Scandinavia and Europe created a rich cultural mix. Although we initially spoke only German at home, exposure to these different cultures was part of my children's upbringing in New Jersey.
implicit_text: Another Scandinavian country, known for its flat lands and maritime history, was represented by immigrants in our small town neighborhood. Living among people from that nation and others from northern Europe created a rich cultural mix. Although we initially spoke only our native tongue at home, exposure to these different cultures was part of my children's upbringing in the neighboring state.

explicit_text: Italian immigrants were also part of the diverse community in Boonton where my family lived. While we didn't socialize much with non-German families at first, the children eventually made friends at school, including those from Italian backgrounds. This mix of cultures made Boonton a true melting pot during that time.
implicit_text: Immigrants from the southern European peninsula known for its boot-shaped geography were also part of the diverse community in the small town where my family lived. While we didn't socialize much with non-native families at first, the children eventually made friends at school, including those from backgrounds tied to that Mediterranean country. This mix of cultures made the town a true melting pot during that time.
